payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Source elements present in the ledger.

original_descriptions: Weekly earnings summary for child stakeholders
Child performance report for compensation distribution
Enable children to claim and monitor task completion for weekly pocket money allocation
Enable children to access weekly earnings summaries for motivational purposes
System deployment nodes specified for iOS mobile platform environment.
System deployment nodes specified for Android mobile platform environment.
System deployment nodes specified for iOS mobile platform environment.
System deployment nodes specified for Windows desktop platform environment.

payload.evaluated_scope: All input material in this project

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Task inventory with completion status and availability attributes
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference tasks as strategic things that require tracking, claiming, and status management

payload.description: Compensated task assignment with associated monetary value
payload.justification: SG050 identifies task assignments as strategic entities with compensation attributes

payload.description: Child performance and earnings report for compensation distribution
payload.justification: SG052 implies earnings summary as a strategic entity that aggregates compensation information | SG053 identifies performance reporting as a strategic entity supporting parental decision-making

payload.description: Longitudinal task history record
payload.justification: SG054 references historical task data as a strategic entity for accountability and analysis

payload.description: Weekly pocket money cycle for task completion and earnings tracking
payload.justification: Both signals explicitly reference weekly cadence as a strategic temporal cycle for the enterprise

payload.description: Child as task claimant and earnings recipient
payload.justification: Multiple signals identify children as strategic actors who claim tasks, view inventory, and access earnings

payload.description: Parent as task definer and compensation distributor
payload.justification: SG050 and SG053 establish parents as strategic role responsible for defining tasks and distributing compensation

payload.description: Enable children to claim and monitor task completion and access weekly earnings summaries for pocket money allocation and motivational purposes
payload.justification: SG049 expresses a fundamental strategic purpose (enabling task claiming and tracking) which maps to Goal classification at the Planner level | SG052 identifies a motivational goal representing strategic intent to support child engagement through earnings visibility

payload.description: Empower parents to establish compensated task assignments with defined monetary values
payload.justification: SG050 articulates parental motivation as a strategic goal for defining task-compensation relationships

payload.description: Provide children transparent access to task inventory status and availability
payload.justification: SG051 captures the strategic purpose of visibility and transparency for child stakeholders

payload.description: Enable parents to access performance reporting for accurate compensation decision-making
payload.justification: SG053 establishes a strategic need for parental access to performance data supporting compensation processes

payload.description: Provide stakeholders longitudinal task history for accountability and trend analysis
payload.justification: SG054 indicates a strategic requirement for historical data access supporting accountability objectives

payload.description: Task Claiming: The business process by which children select and associate themselves with completed tasks to earn monetary rewards.
payload.justification: SG290 establishes the core concept of children claiming tasks; SG292 specifies the linkage mechanism.

payload.description: Task Specification: The capability enabling parents to define tasks and assign monetary values.
payload.justification: SG291 directly describes the parent capability to create and value tasks.

payload.description: Weekly Earnings Aggregation: The calculation function that totals monetary rewards earned by a child within a weekly cycle.
payload.justification: SG294 defines the aggregation capability as a distinct functional capability.

payload.description: Parent Reporting: The capability providing parents with visibility into child-task-earnings relationships to facilitate payment processing.
payload.justification: SG295 describes a specific reporting capability for payment fulfillment support.

payload.description: Weekly Task Reset Rule: Task availability status is automatically reset at the beginning of each weekly cycle.
payload.justification: SG296 explicitly defines a cyclical business rule governing task state transitions.

payload.description: Historical Data Retention Rule: Task completion records must be retained and remain accessible beyond the current period.
payload.justification: SG297 mandates a retention policy as a normative business rule.

payload.description: Task: A defined unit of work assigned within the household with an associated monetary value that can be claimed upon completion.
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference task entities as the core business object with attributes (monetary value) and states (available, completed).

payload.description: Monetary Value: A currency amount assigned to each task representing the reward for completion.
payload.justification: SG291 explicitly defines monetary value as an attribute that parents specify for task entities.

payload.description: Child: A family member authorized to claim and complete tasks for monetary rewards.
payload.justification: Child is consistently referenced as a primary entity that interacts with tasks and accumulates earnings.

payload.description: Parent: A family member authorized to create tasks, assign values, and fulfill payment obligations.
payload.justification: Parent is the entity with administrative capabilities over tasks and payment fulfillment.

payload.description: Task Claim Relationship: Associates a completed task with a specific child for reward attribution.
payload.justification: SG292 defines the linkage between completed tasks and children; SG290 describes the claiming mechanism.

payload.description: Weekly Earnings Total: An aggregated monetary amount representing all rewards earned by a child within a weekly period.
payload.justification: SG294 describes weekly aggregation as a distinct calculated entity visible to child actors.

payload.description: Historical Task Record: Archived task completion data retained for reference across temporal periods.
payload.justification: SG297 mandates retention of task data as a distinct entity type maintained for historical access.

payload.description: Weekly Business Cycle: A recurring seven-day period defining the temporal scope for task availability and earnings aggregation.
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference weekly cycles as the fundamental temporal unit for business operations.

payload.description: Weekly Boundary Trigger: The temporal point at which task availability resets and new earning periods commence.
payload.justification: SG296 defines the weekly boundary as a specific trigger event for state transitions.

payload.description: Task Completion Event: The business-significant moment when a task transitions to completed status and becomes claimable.
payload.justification: Signals distinguish between available and completed tasks, indicating completion is a temporal event.

payload.description: Child Actor: Family member who views available tasks, claims completed work, and monitors earned rewards.
payload.justification: Child is consistently described as an actor with specific visibility and interaction capabilities.

payload.description: Parent Actor: Family member who creates tasks, assigns values, reviews earnings reports, and fulfills payment obligations.
payload.justification: Parent is described as an actor with administrative and oversight responsibilities.

payload.description: Task Creator Role: The responsibility for defining and valuing task entities within the system.
payload.justification: SG291 describes a distinct role capability separate from the parent actor identity.

payload.description: Payment Fulfiller Role: The responsibility for processing and executing monetary payments based on earned rewards.
payload.justification: SG295 describes payment fulfillment as a distinct business role requiring reporting support.

payload.description: Track Monetary Rewards: Enable accurate recording and visibility of earned amounts based on completed task claims.
payload.justification: SG290 establishes reward tracking as the core business objective; SG294 supports this with aggregation.

payload.description: Facilitate Payment Fulfillment: Provide parents with information necessary to execute monetary payments to children.
payload.justification: SG295 explicitly states the purpose of parent reporting as supporting payment fulfillment.

payload.description: Transparency Principle: Children should have visibility into both available opportunities and their earned rewards within the current period.
payload.justification: SG293 defines visibility requirements suggesting a principle of transparent access to relevant information.

payload.description: Task Availability Scope: Child visibility is limited to tasks within the current temporal period.
payload.justification: SG293 specifies temporal scope as a constraint on visibility rather than an open-ended view.

payload.description: Historical Accountability Principle: Task completion records must be preserved to support retrospective review and verification.
payload.justification: SG297's retention mandate suggests an underlying principle of maintaining historical accountability.

payload.description: Aggregation function computing weekly total earnings from completed task records per child user.
payload.justification: Signal describes temporal computation logic transforming raw task completion data into weekly aggregated amounts.

payload.description: Reporting function generating aggregated views of child earnings and task completion metrics for parent users.
payload.justification: Signal describes interface logic providing computed summary data, representing a distinct reporting function.

payload.description: State reset function restoring task availability status at the conclusion of each weekly cycle.
payload.justification: Signal specifies periodic restoration logic that transforms task state, constituting a discrete functional operation.

payload.description: Completion linkage rule mandating association of task completion events to corresponding child entity records.
payload.justification: Signal describes normative association logic that constrains how completion events must be recorded, representing a business rule.

payload.description: Access control rule restricting child user visibility to authorized task collections only.
payload.justification: Data visibility requirements constitute logical access rules governing information availability by user type.

payload.description: Task entity representing assignable work items with completion status and monetary value properties.
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference task data structures including completion state, earnings attributes, and availability status, indicating a core logical entity.

payload.description: Child entity representing minor users with associated earnings accumulation and task assignment relationships.
payload.justification: Child is referenced as a data subject with access patterns, earnings aggregation, and parental oversight requirements.

payload.description: Parent entity representing adult users with oversight privileges over child task and earnings data.
payload.justification: Parent is identified as a distinct logical actor entity with aggregated reporting access rights.

payload.description: Earned amount attribute capturing accumulated monetary value for completed tasks within a temporal period.
payload.justification: Earnings are explicitly mentioned as computed and aggregated values associated with child entities.

payload.description: Task completion association linking child entities to completed task entities with temporal markers.
payload.justification: Signal describes logical linkage rules between completion events and child entities, indicating a relationship structure.

payload.description: Task assignment relationship connecting parent and child entities through task collections with visibility constraints.
payload.justification: Access patterns imply a logical relationship structure governing which child users can view which task collections.

payload.description: Task completion event signaling the transition of task entity state from pending to completed status.
payload.justification: Completion is referenced as a state transition point that triggers association and aggregation processes.

payload.description: Weekly cycle defining the recurring temporal period for earnings computation and task availability reset.
payload.justification: Both signals explicitly reference weekly periodicity as a temporal boundary for computation and state management.

payload.description: Weekly cycle completion trigger initiating task availability state reset operations.
payload.trigger_condition: At conclusion of weekly cycle period
payload.justification: Signal describes behavior activated at a specific temporal boundary, representing a time-based trigger condition.

payload.description: Historical data persistence constraint requiring retention of completed task records for temporal reporting.
payload.justification: Signal mandates logical persistence requirements that constrain system design, representing a data retention constraint.

payload.description: Temporal aggregation principle establishing weekly boundaries as the standard unit for earnings presentation.
payload.justification: Signal describes a design choice establishing weekly periods as the canonical timeframe for financial reporting to users.

payload.description: Specific operating system versions designated as deployment targets for the platform.
payload.justification: Operating system versions represent physical node specifications in the deployment infrastructure. At the Builder level, these are concrete technical platforms that host the system components.

payload.description: Technical requirement limiting platform deployment to approved operating system versions.
payload.justification: The normative nature of the signal explicitly indicates a compatibility constraint that restricts deployment choices. This represents a technical standard that must be enforced at the physical implementation level.

payload.description: Weekly operational cycle governs task claiming execution period and earned amount total access timing.
payload.justification: SG542 establishes weekly boundary timing; SG539 references temporal basis for operations; combined to specify the detailed recurring cycle for implementer-level scheduling.

payload.description: Weekly task availability reset event occurs at cycle boundary enabling new claiming operations.
payload.justification: SG544 specifies timing of discrete state change (reset) that triggers availability for new operations; classified as Event rather than Cycle due to discrete action occurrence.

payload.description: System deployment nodes specified for iOS mobile platform environment.
payload.justification: SG545 identifies iOS as distinct deployment platform; separated into individual node specifications at implementer level for detailed deployment specification. | SG545 identifies Android as distinct deployment platform; separated into individual node specifications at implementer level for detailed deployment specification. | SG545 identifies Windows as distinct deployment platform; separated into individual node specifications at implementer level for detailed deployment specification.

payload.description: Child user interface actor executes task claiming operations and views earned amount totals within weekly operational boundaries.
payload.justification: SG539 and SG541 both describe operational capabilities of child actors; combined to form complete actor specification at implementer level showing both execution and visibility interfaces.

payload.description: Parent user interface actor performs task configuration operations, assigns monetary values, and accesses distribution reports.
payload.justification: SG540 and SG543 describe distinct but related parent actor operations; unified into single actor specification detailing configuration and reporting interface requirements.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Expresses the fundamental purpose of enabling children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks weekly.
Articulates parental motivation to define compensated task assignments with associated monetary values.
Captures the purpose of providing children visibility into available and completed task inventory.
Identifies the motivational goal of enabling children to view their weekly earnings summary.
Establishes parental need to access child performance reporting for accurate compensation distribution purposes.
Indicates stakeholder requirement for longitudinal task history access supporting accountability or trend analysis.
Section header indicating the commencement of conceptual system description.
Establishes the core business concept of children claiming completed tasks for monetary reward tracking.
Defines the parent capability to specify task entities with associated monetary value attributes.
Specifies the relationship between completed task entities and child entities through linkage.
Defines child actor visibility requirements for both available and completed task entities within temporal scope.
Establishes weekly aggregation capability for earned monetary totals visible to child actors.
Defines parent reporting capability displaying child-task-earnings relationships to support payment fulfillment.
Specifies the cyclical business rule for task availability reset at weekly temporal boundaries.
Mandates retention and accessibility of historical task data entities across temporal periods.
Logical association rule defining how task completion events are linked to child entities.
Data visibility requirements specifying logical access patterns for child users to task collections.
Temporal aggregation logic defining weekly computation and presentation of earned amounts to children.
Logical reporting interface providing parents aggregated view of child earnings and task completion data.
Periodic state reset logic defining weekly cycle behavior for task availability restoration.
Data retention requirement mandating logical persistence and accessibility of historical task records.
Platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete operating system versions for deployment.
Identifies children as operational actors who execute task claiming activities throughout the week on a temporal basis.
Identifies parents as operational actors responsible for task configuration and monetary value assignment activities.
Defines operational visibility requirements for child actors viewing available and completed tasks within weekly cycles.
Establishes weekly operational schedule boundary when child actors can access earned amount totals.
Defines parent operational role in accessing reports to execute pocket money distribution activities.
Specifies weekly operational cycle timing when task availability resets for claiming operations.
Defines deployment platform locations specifying where the system operates across iOS, Android, and Windows environments.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Signal elements present in the ledger.
